claims.claim: Users of mod browsers find list-only layouts inefficient because mods 'take up half of screen space' and require excessive scrolling, making a tile/grid toggle a high-priority UX feature.
claims.quote: having a ui that doesn't really show much on your screen and have the mods take up half of screen space is isn't really efficient. By having a way to choose what ui suits people the best like Curseforge will improve the experience of the app drastically.

claims.claim: CurseForge's tile/grid view is cited as the concrete reference implementation for mod browser card density toggles, and its absence in Modrinth is described as the single blocking feature preventing full adoption.
claims.quote: I wish there was a way to configure it just like Curseforge like how you can change it to tiles which are much easier on my eyes and also look prettier and also when browsing for mods. And is the one feature Modrinth is lacking before I can fully commit to using the app and ditching Curseforge for good.

claims.claim: A user-desired tile layout for mod browsers should include a configurable tile size (small/large) as a required sub-feature of any grid view implementation, not an optional enhancement.
claims.quote: Also I forgot to mention but having a way to change the size of the tiles like in curseforge would be greatly appreciated and is basically required for this feature.

claims.claim: Users expect the tile/grid toggle to apply to two distinct surfaces: the mod library/modpack view and the mod search/browse view — both contexts need the same density control.
claims.quote: My solution is having a configuration switch between tiles and list ui format just like Curseforge. For both when you're in your project and when you're browsing for mods in the app.

claims.claim: Card shape preferences in mod browsers include both rounded-corner squares and boxy (sharp-corner) icon styles as distinct options users want to choose between.
claims.quote: Preferably maybe there could also be a option as well for boxy icons and squares with rounded corners.
